title: Phase 3 Remaining Work — Architecture Plan
session_context: ses_next--phase-3-completion handoff reviewed; 4 exploration tasks completed covering storage layer, tool patterns, roadmap files, and PG schema/CI infrastructure.

what_was_expected_vs_found.found: ALL 5 tools fully implemented in packages/opencode/src/tool/roadmap-{init,progress,next,deprecate,prioritize}.ts AND registered in registry.ts (lines 309-310, 485-489, 571-575). Production-ready.
what_was_expected_vs_found.impact: No implementation work needed. Only verification (typecheck) and roadmap status update.

what_was_expected_vs_found.found: Fully implemented in packages/opencode/src/tool/session-diff.ts AND registered in registry.ts (lines 316, 508, 594). Supports summary and full formats.
what_was_expected_vs_found.impact: No implementation work needed. Only verification and roadmap status update.

what_was_expected_vs_found.expected: not_started — needs migration runner for dual SQLite+Pg
what_was_expected_vs_found.found: Core logic fully implemented in packages/opencode/src/storage/migrate.ts with runMigrations(), dialect detection (OPENCODE_DATABASE_DIALECT env), SQLite path via drizzle bun-sqlite migrator, PG path via applyMigrations() from db.pg.ts (supports both PGlite and node-postgres), and CLI entry point with --pg/--sqlite/--db-path/--conn-string flags. HOWEVER, NOT wired into startup — runMigrations() is never called from db.ts, bootstrap.ts, or any initialization path.
what_was_expected_vs_found.impact: Core logic exists. Need one-line integration call in startup path. The `skipMigrations` runtime flag exists but no caller.

what_was_expected_vs_found.found: No implementation exists. Tool name `lesson_register` referenced in 16+ locations across authority.ts (5 roles) and role-contracts.ts (5 lane states). No .ts file, no .txt file. Confirmed missing.
what_was_expected_vs_found.impact: Must be built from scratch following existing tool pattern.

root_causes.hypothesis: CI-004 and CI-006 were implemented in a prior session but the roadmap active.v1.json was never updated to reflect completion
evidence: All 5 roadmap tools exist at packages/opencode/src/tool/roadmap-*.ts with full implementations
registry.ts imports and registers all 6 of these tools
active.v1.json shows CI-004, CI-006 as 'not_started' with 0% completion

root_causes.hypothesis: PG-002 migration runner code exists but startup integration was deferred or overlooked
evidence: migrate.ts has full runMigrations() supporting both dialects and CLI
db.ts has skipMigrations flag but never calls runMigrations()
bootstrap.ts is a thin wrapper over InstanceRuntime, no migration call
No caller of runMigrations() exists anywhere in the startup path

root_causes.hypothesis: CI-005 lesson_register was planned but never implemented; only the tool name was reserved
evidence: Tool name `lesson_register` appears in role-contracts.ts and authority.ts allowed tool lists
Blueprint context_summary mentions it but next_step says 'Done. Verify lessons are being recorded'

fixes.description: Create lesson_register tool (CI-005) following existing tool pattern with Effect/Schema, AppFileSystem, and InstanceState
fixes.before: No lesson_register tool exists. Internal references in authority.ts and role-contracts.ts point to a non-existent tool.
fixes.after: lesson_register tool fully implemented with record/read/list actions, stored at .rig/lessons/lessons.v1.jsonl (append-only), registered in tool registry.

change_details.lesson-register.ts: ~140 lines following the exact pattern of roadmap-init.ts: Effect.gen init, AppFileSystem + InstanceState deps, Schema.Struct parameters, execute returns ExecuteResult. Three actions: record (validates category+insight+context, appends JSONL), read (filters by session or category, returns sorted by recency), list (categories with counts).
change_details.lesson-register.txt: Single line: 'Record or read cross-session lessons — one-sentence insights organized by category for future sessions.'
change_details.registry.ts: 3 changes: (1) import LessonRegisterTool from './lesson-register', (2) add 'lesson_register' to BUILTIN_TOOL_IDS, (3) add lesson_register: Tool.init(lessonRegister) in the layer, (4) add tool.lesson_register to the builtin tool list.

affected_files: packages/opencode/src/tool/registry.ts (+4 lines: import, BUILTIN_TOOL_ID, init, list)
impact.backward_compatibility: Fully backward-compatible. New tool only. Existing references in authority.ts and role-contracts.ts will now resolve correctly.

fixes.surgical_precision: Creates 2 new files + 1 surgical 4-line insertion in registry.ts. No restructuring.
risks: Risk: .rig/lessons/ directory may not exist — mitigated by fs.ensureDir() before write
Risk: The tool ID lesson_register may conflict with a future lesson_read tool — mitigated by using a single tool with action parameter
Risk: JSONL file growing unbounded — mitigated by low expected volume (lessons are infrequent)
validation: Step 1: bun typecheck passes with no new errors
Step 2: Verify tool appears in the builtin tool list by checking registry output
Step 3: Pre-seed 5 lessons from the session using the tool
Step 4: Read back lessons and verify the JSONL file exists at .rig/lessons/lessons.v1.jsonl

fixes.description: Update roadmap active.v1.json to reflect actual completion status of CI-004, CI-006, and PG-002
fixes.before: CI-004: not_started (0%), CI-005: not_started (0%), CI-006: not_started (0%), PG-002: not_started (0%)
fixes.after: CI-004: completed (100%), CI-006: completed (100%), PG-002: core complete with startup wiring note (85%), CI-005: in_progress (the lessons register being built in fix-A).

change_details.CI-004: status: 'completed', completion_pct: 100, add session note with evidence (files exist, registered)
change_details.CI-006: status: 'completed', completion_pct: 100, add session note with evidence
change_details.PG-002: status: 'in_progress', completion_pct: 85, add note: 'Core runner implemented. Needs startup wiring.'
change_details.CI-005: status: 'in_progress', completion_pct: 0 → will be updated after fix-A

tests_to_run: Verify JSON is valid (cat active.v1.json | python3 -m json.tool)

fixes.surgical_precision: 4 field changes in a single JSON file. Each is a status + completion_pct + session note entry.
risks: Risk: The blueprint (opencode-desktop-phase3-roadmap.v1.json) is not auto-updated — this is intentional; blueprint is the authoritative source, active is the working copy.
Risk: If another agent edits active.v1.json simultaneously, partial overwrite could occur. Mitigated by editing as a whole document, not fragment.
validation: Step 2: Verify roadmap_next shows updated priorities correctly

fixes.description: Wire PG-002 migration runner into database initialization path — call runMigrations() during app startup when skipMigrations is false
files: packages/opencode/src/storage/migrate.ts (MODIFY — small change to support programmatic invocation from Effect context)
fixes.before: db.ts Client() creates the database via init(dbPath) but never calls runMigrations(). The skipMigrations runtime flag exists but nothing checks it for an actual migration invocation.
fixes.after: On first database initialization (loaded=false → true transition), if !flags.skipMigrations, call runMigrations() with the resolved dbPath. For the async nature, fire-and-forget via a background promise, or switch Client() to async.

change_details.db.ts: Add `import { runMigrations } from './migrate'` and call it asynchronously after init() when !flags.skipMigrations. Since Client() is currently synchronous, wrap in a fire-and-forget promise with error logging (non-blocking).
change_details.alternative: The cleaner approach: make the app startup async (bootstrap.ts or InstanceRuntime.load) call runMigrations() before providing the context. This avoids making Client() async.
change_details.recommended: Add runMigrations() call in the bootstrap or InstanceRuntime.load path — it's an initialization concern, not a per-request concern.

affected_files: packages/opencode/src/storage/db.ts (add import + fire-and-forget call after init)
OR packages/opencode/src/cli/bootstrap.ts (add runMigrations call before context.provide)
tests_to_run: Verify migration is NOT called when OPENCODE_SKIP_MIGRATIONS=true
Verify migration IS called when OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL is set
impact.backward_compatibility: Migration is idempotent (Drizzle migrator handles this). Adding it to startup is safe but may add ~100-500ms on first startup for migration checks.

fixes.surgical_precision: 2-3 line addition. Import runMigrations, call it with resolved dbPath/dialect, wrap in non-blocking promise.
risks: Risk: PGlite (:memory:) may not need migrations because Drizzle ORM queries auto-create tables. Mitigated: runMigrations handles this gracefully — if no migration files apply, it's a no-op.
Risk: In production, migrations should be controlled (not auto-applied). Mitigated: the skipMigrations flag exists for this purpose.

Risk: Dual migration application (SQLite migration folder + PG migration folder) could conflict if both exist. Mitigated: dialect detection picks the right one based on OPENCODE_DATABASE_DIALECT or OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL.
HIGH RISK - DEFERRED: The exact integration point needs validation. Recommend deferring this fix to a dedicated execution subagent that can test the startup flow.
validation: Step 2: Run app with OPENCODE_DB=:memory: — verify migration doesn't error
Step 3: Run app with OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL set — verify migration runs against PG

lane_1.title: Lesson register tool + pre-seed 5 lessons
lane_1.boundary: lesson_register tool exists, registered, typechecks, and can record/read lessons
lane_1.consumer_purpose: Agents need cross-session pattern memory — record one-sentence insights at session end and read them at session start

lane_2.title: Update roadmap active.v1.json statuses
lane_2.boundary: active.v1.json reflects actual completion status of CI-004, CI-005, CI-006, PG-002
lane_2.consumer_purpose: roadmap_next and downstream tooling need accurate state to recommend next actions

lane_3.title: Wire migration runner into startup
lane_3.boundary: runMigrations() is called during app startup when !skipMigrations, with correct dialect detection
lane_3.consumer_purpose: Database tables must exist before first query — currently depends on manual migration or PGlite auto-create behavior

parallel_execution.notes: lane_1 (CI-005) and lane_2 (roadmap update) have zero file overlap and can run in parallel. lane_3 (PG-002 wiring) is independent of CI-005 but depends on accurate roadmap state for tracking — can also run in parallel but deferred due to higher risk.

rollback_plan: revert each fix in reverse order: (1) remove lesson_register import from registry.ts + delete 2 files, (2) restore active.v1.json from git, (3) revert db.ts/migrate.ts changes via git checkout
